The San Antonio Spurs frontloaded Julian Champagnie's extension. This will give San Antonio a bit more flexibility later, as young players like Victor Wembanyama and Stephon Castle sign extensions.

items.title: San Antonio Spurs after signing Tobias Harris, Harrison Barnes, and Julian Champagnie.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
San Antonio Spurs after signing Tobias Harris, Harrison Barnes, and Julian Champagnie.

They still have the bi-annual but may use last roster spots on minimums instead to avoid the luxury tax.

All that's left to do this offseason: Sign Victor Wembanyama to a maximum extension.

items.title: Prophetic words regarding Julian Champagnie from Wemby back in January after a game in Boston:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Prophetic words regarding Julian Champagnie from Wemby back in January after a game in Boston:

“The front office, the coaching staff, everybody believes in him and he’s just capitalizing on that. It’s like we can’t  do without him now. He’s a big part of this. He’s indispensable.”

items.title: Midway through last season, Julian Champagnie received a ringing endorsement from an important source.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Midway through last season, Julian Champagnie received a ringing endorsement from an important source.

"He’s indispensable, Victor Wembanyama said.

The Spurs agreed, signing the forward on Monday to a three-year, $45 million pact, a league source confirmed.

items.title: A league source confirmed that Julian Champagnie plans to sign a new three-year, $45 million pact with the Spurs through 2028-29 after the club declined his $3 million team option.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
A league source confirmed that Julian Champagnie plans to sign a new three-year, $45 million pact with the Spurs through 2028-29 after the club declined his $3 million team option.

First reported by ESPN's Shams Charania.

items.title: Spurs salary cap situation after re-signing Julian Champagnie.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs salary cap situation after re-signing Julian Champagnie.

They still have plenty of money to spend despite giving him a significant raise.

They could still use the $15 million mid-level exception and make a big consolidation trade.

They can trade up to 4 firsts.
